title: Sort an Array
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: C proposal: the minimum-memory champion (in-place heapsort). Recommended and speed proposals are in C++ (see crossReferences).

variants.languageReason: Smallest runtime/stdlib baseline -> minimum reported memory; manual control enables a truly in-place, allocation-free heapsort.
variants.approachJustification: In-place heapsort with an iterative sift-down (no auxiliary array, no recursion stack).

reasoningSummary: In-place heapsort sorts with zero auxiliary memory and no recursion stack - the minimum-memory proposal; it makes no assumption about the value range.
